channel.title: Gamification on CEDIS
channel.description: Recent content in Gamification on CEDIS

item.title: OctaAnalysis Project
item.description: Digital platform by CEDIS/UnB to support the design, management, and analysis of gamified educational and collaborative experiences with integrated analytics.

item.title: Products
item.description: Web application developed as a bachelor’s thesis with academic support from CEDIS for gamified learning about code smells, combining conceptual guides, interactive exercises, hints, treats, statistics, and progression.

item.title: Gamification
item.description: Gamification is a process that uses elements, techniques, and game design mechanics in contexts not related to games, aiming to increase user motivation, participation, and engagement. Gamification has been applied in various fields, such as education, marketing, health, and corporate training.

item.title: Products
item.description: Gamified experience for the Software Requirements course at UnB — missions, storyline, and motivational tracks inspired by Octalysis, combining structured gamification with Project-Based Learning (PBL).

item.title: Products
item.description: Web platform developed as a bachelor’s thesis at the University of Brasilia to support the gamification of Moodle-based courses, combining motivational-profile analysis, technique guidance, plugin cataloguing, and explanatory content about gamification, Octalysis, and Moodle.

item.title: Products
item.description: Android mobile app developed as a bachelor’s thesis with academic support from CEDIS for teaching data structures through augmented reality and gamification, featuring themed rooms, mini missions, and an achievement passport.

item.title: Products
item.description: Gamified application developed as a bachelor’s thesis with academic support from CEDIS for managing and motivating agile teams, integrated with GitHub as a GitHub App with a metrics dashboard, badges, and social feedback.

item.title: OctaAnalysis Project
item.description: Ongoing CEDIS project focused on the research, design, validation, and continuous evolution of methods and infrastructures for gamification and learning analytics, with OctaAnalysis as its resulting product.

item.title: Products
item.description: An educational platform for tracking, analyzing, and gamifying classes, with academic support from CEDIS, grounded in a semester-start diagnostic instrument validated across undergraduate courses.

item.title: Products
item.description: An AI API for academic performance prediction in gamified environments, complementing the EduTrack platform, with academic support from CEDIS.

item.title: Software for Good
item.description: Software for Good (SpB) is a collective—a community of people and organizations, meanings, actions, solutions, feelings, and energies aligned to generate good through the use of software.

item.title: Super r
item.description: Educational project, part of the ‘Software Requirements’ course, an educational innovation developed by CEDIS at the University of Brasília.

item.title: Canal Ciência Project
item.description: A research project in partnership with IBICT and funded by FINATEC, aimed at enhancing the user experience of the Canal Ciência through the use of gamification.

item.title: People
item.description: Full Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the undergraduate Software Engineering program and the Graduate Program in Applied Computing.

item.title: HCI Journey Project
item.description: Teaching project, “Human-Computer Interaction” course, an educational innovation promoted by CEDIS in the Software Engineering course at the University of Brasília

item.title: Feudal Dawn Project
item.description: Teaching project, “Software Development” course, an educational innovation promoted by CEDIS at the University of Brasília.

item.title: Products
item.description: Galactic Journey, a Gamification for the Canal Ciência Portal.

item.title: People
item.description: Associate Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the Software Engineering undergraduate program and the Graduate Program in Applied Computing with a focus on requirements, human aspects, and software engineering education.

item.title: Products
item.description: Technological product recorded in 2025 for child sexual abuse prevention through gamification and an educational application for children.

item.title: Products
item.description: 2025 technological product focused on preventing childhood violence through a gamified application for parents and educators.

item.title: People
item.description: The project is to create the GCMP (Gamified Chatbot Development Process), a structured method for developing gamified chatbots.

item.title: People
item.description: Undergraduate in Software Engineering, University of Brasília

item.title: People
item.description: Undergraduate in Software Engineering, University of Brasília

item.title: People
item.description: Undergraduate in Software Engineering, University of Brasília

item.title: People
item.description: Undergraduate in Software Engineering, University of Brasília

item.title: People
item.description: Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the Software Engineering undergraduate program on software quality, digital government, and software engineering education.

item.title: Software Engineering students' thesis gives rise to Git Ranking, a gamified application for agile teams on GitHub
item.description: A bachelor's thesis supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas resulted in Git Ranking, a gamified application integrated with GitHub.

item.title: Paper on EduTrack's diagnostic instrument accepted at SBIE 2026
item.description: Paper (submission #27258) authored by Sergio Freitas, Daniel Rodrigues da Rocha, Davi Rodrigues da Rocha, and Mylena Angélica Silva Farias was accepted at SBIE 2026.

item.title: Paper on DevDog accepted at the SBES 2026 Tools Track
item.description: A paper stemming from Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior's bachelor's thesis was accepted at SBES 2026, supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas and co-supervised by Prof. André Lanna.

item.title: Software Engineering students' thesis gives rise to Atloria, an augmented reality app for teaching data structures
item.description: A bachelor's thesis supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas resulted in Atloria, an educational augmented reality app.

item.title: DevDog: undergraduate thesis turns code smells into gamified learning
item.description: Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis that gave rise to DevDog, a gamified web application for teaching code smell identification.

item.title: OctaAnalysis platform expands CEDIS's portfolio of digital innovation
item.description: OctaAnalysis is a CEDIS platform focused on the design, management, and analytical monitoring of gamified experiences.

item.title: UnB’s Software Engineering Program hosts Gamification Team from Banco do Brasil
item.description: UnB’s Software Engineering Program hosts Gamification Team from Banco do Brasil

item.title: CEDIS at UnB: recognition and innovation
item.description: CEDIS celebrates the achievement of Professor Sergio Freitas, who received an Honorable Mention in the Annual Undergraduate Teaching Innovation Award (DEG/UnB), in the category Use of technological resources and production of innovative teaching material.

item.title: Gamifying Scrum: an undergraduate thesis maps the literature and drafts guidelines for the field
item.description: Denniel Roriz Lima defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis running a bibliometric and thematic review on the use of gamification in agile processes with Scrum, and proposing practical guidelines for implementation.

item.title: Article on gamified chatbot development process presented at international conference
item.description: Article presented at IntelliSys 2024, in Amsterdam, Netherlands.

item.title: Gamifying Moodle without becoming a programmer: an undergraduate thesis delivers a guide and tool for instructors
item.description: Antonio Rangel Chaves and Vitor Diniz Pagani Vieira Ribeiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis lowering the entry barrier for teachers who want to gamify Moodle courses — with a guide and a practical tool.

item.title: Canal Ciência project wins best paper award at international conference
item.description: Paper was presented at the 24th ICCSA in Hanoi, Vietnam.

item.title: Software engineering student and CEDIS fellow presents paper at international conference
item.description: Paper presented at the 24th ICCSA in Hanoi, Vietnam.

item.title: GCMP: a dissertation systematizes how to build gamified chatbots
item.description: Arthur Rocha Temporim de Lacerda defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program a dissertation proposing GCMP — the Gamified Chatbot Management Process — a method to orchestrate the end-to-end development of gamified chatbots.

item.title: UFPA Student Undertakes Internship at CEDIS
item.description: UFPA student undertakes an internship at CEDIS focusing on gamification for literacy

item.title: Gamifying a public enterprise: dissertation studies how and why the strategy (sometimes) engages
item.description: Luciana Santos de Assis defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program a dissertation critically analyzing gamification implementation in organizational contexts — through a case study inside a Brazilian public enterprise.

item.title: Latte: a duo trains a gamified chatbot to help write scientific articles
item.description: Luiz Henrique Fernandes Zamprogno and Victor Eduardo Araújo Ribeiro defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis introducing Latte — a gamified chatbot to support the production of scientific articles.

item.title: App supports gamified HCI course: a thesis delivers a tool built for the real classroom
item.description: Ítalo Alves Guimarães defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis implementing an application to support the gamified Human-Computer Interaction course — a piece the very use in class had been asking for.

item.title: Jornada role playing games enter the classroom as a learning process
item.description: João Vitor Lopes de Farias defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis examining the use of RPGs as the backbone of a learning process — combining narrative, roles and challenges with clear pedagogical goals.

item.title: CEDIS Participates in Gamification Project
item.description: Partnership between CEDIS and IBICT launches an innovative gamification project for the Canal Ciência portal

item.title: Police intelligence reports under the gamification lens: a dissertation measures what changes
item.description: Públio Pastrolin Cavalcante defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation evaluating how gamification applied to police intelligence activities affects the quality of reports produced.

item.title: CEDIS Gamification Innovates HCI Teaching at FCTE
item.description: IHC Journey - An RPG adventure in learning the Human-Computer Interaction course.

item.title: CEDIS Gamification Revolutionizes Software Teaching at FCTE
item.description: Innovative gamification by Prof. Sergio Freitas engages Software Engineering students with a playful and effective approach.

item.title: SAMI: an undergraduate thesis creates a system to analyze learning metrics
item.description: João Vitor de Moura Rosa Silva defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis building SAMI — a system for analyzing learning indicator metrics in active-methodology courses.

item.title: $tudy: an undergraduate thesis brings financial education into a gamified system
item.description: Rafael Santos Teodosio defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis designing and implementing $tudy — a gamified system aimed at supporting the financial education of young people.

item.title: University of Heuristics: an undergraduate thesis turns HCI teaching into a serious game
item.description: Cauê Mateus Oliveira and Lucas Midlhey Cardoso Naves defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis turning the study of Nielsen’s heuristics into a game — the University of Heuristics.

item.title: Pixel: an undergraduate thesis gamifies learning of usability criteria
item.description: Érico Maximiano Bandeira defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis introducing Pixel — a gamified tool for learning usability criteria during interface evaluation.

item.title: Which framework to use when gamifying a class? A systematic review draws the map
item.description: Weiller Fernandes Pereira defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis carrying out a systematic literature review on the use of frameworks in building education-oriented gamification projects.

item.title: REQGAME: a dissertation tests gamification for requirements elicitation in a federal public agency
item.description: Ana Cláudia Alves Mendes Araújo defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation applying gamification to the requirements elicitation process in software projects run at a federal public agency.

item.title: A junior enterprise, a live experiment: an undergraduate thesis evaluates gamification applied on the ground
item.description: Ateldy Borges Brasil Filho defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis evaluating gamification applied in a junior enterprise — an organization with rotating teams, short cycles, and high initial motivation.

item.title: Measure to engage: an undergraduate thesis proposes a strategy to improve gamified applications
item.description: Paulo Markes Rodrigues de Oliveira Calado defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing a strategy to identify improvement opportunities in gamified applications — tackling the problem that the initial shine does not always translate into prolonged use.

item.title: Attracting is easy, retaining is gamification: an undergraduate thesis fights churn in an academic social network
item.description: Tiago Ribeiro de Assunção defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis applying gamification to the About Social Network to fight its weak spot: the network attracts new users easily but fails to retain them.

item.title: Math, children, and digital victories: the software that wants to turn a class into a mission
item.description: Leandro Alves Ferreira Santos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing gamified software to support math teaching for children — a territory poorly served by edtech products.

item.title: A tool to help teams build gamification projects without starting from scratch
item.description: Ludimila da Bela Cruz defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing a tool to support teams building gamification projects, anchoring the process in structured motivational elements.

item.title: Algorithms with points: an undergraduate thesis adapts a virtual environment to gamified programming teaching
item.description: Wilker Mesquita de Assunção defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis adapting a virtual learning environment to teach algorithms using gamification techniques.
